key_insight: The paper learns fast approximate solution maps for multiparametric generalized Nash equilibrium problems by training on the Nikaido-Isoda gap instead of expensive full equilibrium labels.
engineering_relevance: This enables near-real-time equilibrium approximation in coupled multi-agent systems, with large speedups over online solvers for optimization and control applications.
method: Use the Nikaido-Isoda gap as a loss so training needs only best-response data.
Replace bilevel best-response optimization with a value-function surrogate to obtain a single-level learning problem.
Extend the approach to standard multiparametric programming and prove existence of continuous parametric GNE selections under strong variational stability.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments across multiple problem classes and states that the trained network achieves speedups of several orders of magnitude over online solvers.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the surrogate loss and stability assumptions, so the method may be sensitive when best-response approximations or variational stability are weak.
paper_title: Learning Approximate Solutions to Multiparametric Generalized Nash Equilibrium Problems
